name: opcua-asyncio (OPC-UA Client/Server)

auth.notes: OPC-UA security modes: None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt; username/password or certificate

performance.notes: asyncio-based; supports subscriptions for push-based data without polling

agent_readiness.notes: OPC-UA client and server in pure Python (asyncio). from asyncua import Client, Server. async with Client('opc.tcp://host:4840') as client: root = client.get_root_node(); var = await client.get_node('ns=2;i=2'). val = await var.read_value(). await var.write_value(42). Subscription: handler = SubHandler(); sub = await client.create_subscription(500, handler). await sub.subscribe_data_change([var]). Server: async with Server() as server: uri = await server.register_namespace('http://myorg.com'); myobj = await server.nodes.objects.add_object(idx, 'MyObject'). Security: await client.set_security_string('Basic256Sha256,SignAndEncrypt,cert.pem,key.pem').

gotchas: LGPL-3.0 — dynamic linking obligation for commercial use
Node addressing (NodeId) uses namespace index + identifier — must know server's namespace map
Subscription handlers run in asyncio event loop — don't block
Security certificate setup is complex but essential for production
Discovery and browsing server address space can be slow for large servers
best_when: Industrial automation: reading/writing PLC/SCADA tags via OPC-UA
Subscription-based push data from industrial equipment
Building OPC-UA servers to expose Python data to SCADA systems
When security (authentication, encryption) matters for industrial connectivity
avoid_when: Simple Modbus devices without OPC-UA support (use pymodbus)
When commercial OPC-UA SDK with vendor support is required
summary: opcua-asyncio provides OPC-UA client/server in Python. async with Client('opc.tcp://host:4840') as c: node = c.get_node('ns=2;i=2'); val = await node.read_value(). Subscriptions for push updates. Server: add_object(), add_variable(). Security: certificate auth, Sign+Encrypt. LGPL-3.0 by FreeOpcUa. Best for industrial OPC-UA integration. Complex node addressing. Security setup required for production. Subscription handlers must not block asyncio.
